ci_support_linux_64_python3.10.____cpyth:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  python:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
python:
- 3.10.* *_cpython
root_base:
- 6.36.8
root_cxx_standard:
- '20'
target_platform:
- linux-64
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version
- - root_base
  - root_cxx_standard

about.description:
HistFitter is a statistical analysis framework designed for performing shape and normalization fits using the HistFactory model within ROOT and RooFit. It enables rapid development of physics analyses by generating histograms from ROOT TTrees according to user-defined configurations describing samples, variables, selections, and systematic uncertainties.

The framework dynamically constructs histograms in memory from configuration files, allowing the histogram-building stage to be executed once and reused across multiple analyses or parameter scans. Generated histograms and corresponding HistFactory XML configurations are written to ROOT files for efficient reuse, avoiding repeated data processing when analysis definitions remain unchanged.

HistFitter integrates histogram production, statistical model construction, and workspace evaluation into a single workflow, supporting large-scale analyses that require constrained nuisance parameters derived from control regions and applied to signal-region fits.
about.summary: HistFitter is a framework for building and performing statistical fits with ROOT HistFactory using configurable histogram workflows.

name: histfitter

about.description:
HistFitter is a statistical analysis framework designed for performing shape and normalization fits using the HistFactory model within ROOT and RooFit. It enables rapid development of physics analyses by generating histograms from ROOT TTrees according to user-defined configurations describing samples, variables, selections, and systematic uncertainties.

The framework dynamically constructs histograms in memory from configuration files, allowing the histogram-building stage to be executed once and reused across multiple analyses or parameter scans. Generated histograms and corresponding HistFactory XML configurations are written to ROOT files for efficient reuse, avoiding repeated data processing when analysis definitions remain unchanged.

HistFitter integrates histogram production, statistical model construction, and workspace evaluation into a single workflow, supporting large-scale analyses that require constrained nuisance parameters derived from control regions and applied to signal-region fits.
about.summary: HistFitter is a framework for building and performing statistical fits with ROOT HistFactory using configurable histogram workflows.

raw_meta_yaml:
context:
  version: "1.4.1"

package:
  name: histfitter
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  url: https://github.com/histfitter/histfitter/archive/refs/tags/v${{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 887e0a5a4b1e898a11f2229494dbbd287e4d567f9f964f6aa6f0059ec4bc0b5c
  patches:
    - patches/0001-install-with-python-sitearch.patch
    - patches/0003-clean-up-installs.patch
    - patches/0004-add-init.py.patch
    - patches/0005-remove-extraneous-caches.patch

build:
  number: 1
  skip:
    - win
  script:
    - cmake ${CMAKE_ARGS}  -S . -B build
    - cmake --build build --clean-first --parallel="${CPU_COUNT}"
    - cmake --install build

requirements:
  build:
    - ${{ compiler('c') }}
    - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
    - ${{ stdlib('c') }}
    - cmake
    - make
    - if: build_platform != target_platform
      then:
        - cross-python_${{ target_platform }}
        - python
        - root_base
  host:
    - root_base
    - root_cxx_standard
    - python
  run:
    - python
    - pyhf

tests:
  - python:
      imports:
        - HistFitter
  - package_contents:
      strict: true
      files:
        # --- core library ---
        - lib/libHistFitter.so
        - lib/libHistFitter.rootmap
        - lib/libHistFitter_rdict.pcm
        # --- headers ---
        - include/histfitter/*.h
        # --- executables ---
        - bin/histfitter
        - bin/setup_histfitter.sh
        - bin/histfitter_env_setup.sh
        # --- key scripts ---
        - bin/histfitter
        - bin/GenerateJSONOutput.py
        - bin/GenerateTreeDescriptionFromJSON.py
        - bin/HypoTest.py
        - bin/PrintFitResult.py
        - bin/PrintFitResultTex.py
        - bin/SysTable.py
        - bin/SysTableTex.py
        - bin/SystRankingPlot.py
        - bin/SystRankingPlotMP.py
        - bin/UpperLimitTable.py
        - bin/UpperLimitTableTex.py
        - bin/YieldsTable.py
        - bin/YieldsTableTex.py
        - bin/contourPlotter.py
        - bin/getFinalFullJER.py
        - bin/harvestHepdataToContours.py
        - bin/harvestToContours.py
        - bin/mergeSysTableBreakdown.py
        - bin/multiplexContours.py
        - bin/multiplexJSON.py
        - bin/plotUpDown.py
        - bin/pull_maker.py
        # --- python package ---
        - lib/python*/site-packages/HistFitter/**
        - share/histfitter/**
        # compiled python (allowed via wildcard)
        - bin/__pycache__/*
        - bin/*.pyc
        - lib/python*/site-packages/HistFitter/__pycache__/*
        - lib/python*/site-packages/HistFitter/**/*.pyc

about:
  homepage: https://github.com/histfitter/histfitter
  summary: 'HistFitter is a framework for building and performing statistical fits with ROOT HistFactory using configurable histogram workflows.'
  description: |
    HistFitter is a statistical analysis framework designed for performing shape and normalization fits using the HistFactory model within ROOT and RooFit. It enables rapid development of physics analyses by generating histograms from ROOT TTrees according to user-defined configurations describing samples, variables, selections, and systematic uncertainties.

    The framework dynamically constructs histograms in memory from configuration files, allowing the histogram-building stage to be executed once and reused across multiple analyses or parameter scans. Generated histograms and corresponding HistFactory XML configurations are written to ROOT files for efficient reuse, avoiding repeated data processing when analysis definitions remain unchanged.

    HistFitter integrates histogram production, statistical model construction, and workspace evaluation into a single workflow, supporting large-scale analyses that require constrained nuisance parameters derived from control regions and applied to signal-region fits.
  license: BSD-2-Clause
  license_file: LICENSE
  documentation: https://twiki.cern.ch/twiki/bin/viewauth/AtlasProtected/SusyFitter
  repository: https://github.com/histfitter/histfitter

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - kratsg
    - matthewfeickert
    - longjon929
